questions.question: What is the primary structural difference between an OLTP (Transaction) Database and an OLAP (Analytical) Data Warehouse?
options: OLTP uses Python, while OLAP relies exclusively on Javascript DOM manipulation.
OLTP databases are highly 'Normalized' to make INSERTS fast. OLAP Data Warehouses intentionally 'Denormalize' data to make massive analytical READs and aggregations incredibly fast.
OLTP databases strictly prevent standard SQL.
questions.answer: OLTP databases are highly 'Normalized' to make INSERTS fast. OLAP Data Warehouses intentionally 'Denormalize' data to make massive analytical READs and aggregations incredibly fast.
questions.explanation: Normalizing data requires slow JOINs. Data Warehouses flatten the data so analysts can query millions of rows smoothly without exploding the CPU.

questions.question: In standard Dimensional Modeling, what differentiates a 'Fact' Table from a 'Dimension' Table?
options: Fact tables contain the massive, rapidly accumulating numerical measurements and transaction data. Dimension tables are smaller descriptive contexts utilized to filter or GROUP BY those facts logically.
Fact tables strictly utilize NoSQL JSON blobs.
Dimension tables explicitly host physical images natively.
questions.answer: Fact tables contain the massive, rapidly accumulating numerical measurements and transaction data. Dimension tables are smaller descriptive contexts utilized to filter or GROUP BY those facts logically.
questions.explanation: Facts are the verbs (A purchase occurred). Dimensions are the nouns (Who bought it, what they bought, uniquely where they bought it).

questions.question: Why is the explicit 'Star Schema' heavily preferred over the 'Snowflake Schema'?
options: The Star Schema keeps Dimension tables completely un-nested securely, natively explicitly strictly ensuring that massive analytical aggregations require ONLY simple 1-level JOINs cleanly.
The Star schema prevents CSS styling.
It forces native garbage collection intelligently.
questions.answer: The Star Schema keeps Dimension tables completely un-nested securely, natively explicitly strictly ensuring that massive analytical aggregations require ONLY simple 1-level JOINs cleanly.
questions.explanation: Snowflaking normalizes the dimensions, effectively creating a massive web of 5-level deep JOIN requirements that slows down the Data Warehouse.
